rationale: The page provides detailed information on the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), specifically the roles of Gram Sabhas and State Governments. It also covers Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs), including their identification criteria and population statistics, and introduces the TALASH initiative for tribal education.
cleanedText: Responsibilities of Gram Sabhas: - Verifying claims through Forest Rights Committees (FRC), based on customary practices and evidence of long-term occupation. - Claims reviewed by Sub-Divisional Level Committees (SDLC) and District Level Committees (DLC) - final approving authority. - Form Community Forest Resource Management Committee (CFRMC) for carrying out conservation and management of wildlife, forest, and biodiversity. - CFRMC prepares Community Forest Resources Management Plan (CFRMP). Role of State Government: State Governments establish committees, issuing notifications, and ensuring the recognition and vesting of forest rights. - They are responsible for creating the State Level Monitoring Committee (SLMC), DLCs, and SDLCs. Important Judicial Pronouncements regarding implementation of FRA: - Orissa Mining Corporation v. Ministry of Environment and Forests case (2013), popularly known as Niyamgiri Case: The court recognised tribal’s cultural, religious and spiritual rights on the Niyamgiri Hill, over Vedanta Company’s claim to exploit the hills for bauxite. 8.7.3. PARTICULARLY VULNERABLE TRIBAL GROUPS (PVTG). Why in the news? Recently, Centre has approved 25 new road projects in Tripura (sanctioned under Road Connectivity component of PM-JANMAN) to improve connectivity in PVTG areas. About PVTGs: - They are among the most marginalized and vulnerable sections among tribal communities. - Ministry of Home Affairs has recognized 75 tribal communities as PVTGs based on recommendations of Dhebar Commission (1960-61). - PVTGs are centrally recognized special category from among Scheduled Tribe. - PVTGs reside in 18 States and UT of A&N Islands. Odisha has highest number of PVTGs. - A recent Centre habitation-level survey estimated the PVTG population to be 45.56 lakh. - Largest populations: Madhya Pradesh (12.28L), Maharashtra (6.2L), Andhra Pradesh (4.9L). Characteristics for identification of PVTGs: - Preservation of pre-agricultural practices. - Hunting and gathering practices. - Decreasing or stagnant population growth. - Relatively low levels of literacy in contrast to other tribal group. 8.7.4. TALASH INITIATIVE. National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS), under Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in partnership with UNICEF India launched TALASH (Tribal Aptitude, Life Skills and Self-Esteem Hub) Initiative. About TALASH Initiative: - It is a national-program to support the all-round development (both educational and personal growth) of students in Eklavya Model Residential Schools (EMRSs). - EMRS is a central sector scheme under Ministry of Tribal Affairs to provide residential schooling to tribal children in blocks having more than 50% of ST population. - Started in 2018-19 to provide quality education at par with Navodaya Vidyalaya to tribal children in.
